item.title: Nchwaning mine
item.description: The N'chwaning mine (or N'Chwaning mines) are located in the Northern Cape Province of South Africa. N'chwaning has one of the largest manganese reserves in South Africa having estimated reserves of 323.2 million tonnes of manganese ore grading 42.5% manganese.[1] There are three shafts, designated as N'Chwaning, N'Chwaning II and N'Chwaning III. The majority of the major mineral finds documented originate from the N'Chwaning II shaft.

item.title: Nazi eugenics
item.description: The social policies of eugenics in Nazi Germany were composed of various ideas about genetics. The racial ideology of Nazism placed the biological improvement of the German people by selective breeding of "Nordic" or "Aryan" traits at its center.[1] These policies were used to justify the involuntary sterilization and mass murder of those deemed "undesirable".

item.title: Simon Handle
item.description: Simon Handle (born 25 January 1993) is a German professional footballer who plays as a right winger for 3. Liga club FC Viktoria Köln.[1]

item.title: Eckhardt
item.description: Eckhardt is a surname and less commonly a given name. Notable people with the name include:

item.title: Jiangnan sizhu
item.description: Jiangnan sizhu (Wu Chinese pronunciation: [koŋ nø sɨ d̥zoʔ]) is a style of traditional Chinese instrumental music from the Jiangnan region of China.[1][2]
